mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal fails to predict direction in backtests with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which sustains corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition occurs but SPX forward 60-day returns are negative in > 55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side constraints, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of stable global commodity prices.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP exceeds 1.5% annualized, indicating robust macroeconomic expansion that supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 1.5% is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of quarters, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and equity returns remains below 0.10 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.3% signals accelerating inflation that forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.3% m/m are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the inflation threshold fails to predict rate hike timing with > 70% accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month payroll growth exceeding 150,000 and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, boosting consumer spending expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor improvement condition yields negative 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction effect between PAYEMS and UNRATE is statistically indistinguishable from zero in predictive models.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production during periods of stable global trade flows indicate healthy manufacturing demand, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during trade stability are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or the signal reverses direction during tariff escalation periods.
